00029 
00030 #ifndef _THREAD_H_
00031 #define _THREAD_H_
00032 
00033 /*
00034  * Definition of a thread.
00035  *
00036  * Note: curthread is defined by <current.h>.
00037  */
00038 
00039 #include <spinlock.h>
00040 #include <threadlist.h>
00041 #include <kern/file.h>
00042 #include <mips/trapframe.h>
00043 
00044 struct addrspace;
00045 struct cpu;
00046 struct vnode;
00047 
00048 /* get machine-dependent defs */
00049 #include <machine/thread.h>
00050 
00051 #define STACK_OFFSET    (512)
00052 
00053 #define MAX_THREAD      1024
00054 
00055 /* Size of kernel stacks; must be power of 2 */
00056 #define STACK_SIZE 4096
00057 
00058 /* Mask for extracting the stack base address of a kernel stack pointer */
00059 #define STACK_MASK  (~(vaddr_t)(STACK_SIZE-1))
00060 
00061 /* Macro to test if two addresses are on the same kernel stack */
00062 #define SAME_STACK(p1, p2)     (((p1) & STACK_MASK) == ((p2) & STACK_MASK))
00063 
00064 /* States a thread can be in. */
00065 typedef enum {
00066         S_RUN,          /* running */
00067         S_READY,        /* ready to run */
00068         S_SLEEP,        /* sleeping */
00069         S_ZOMBIE,       /* zombie; exited but not yet deleted */
00070 } threadstate_t;
00071 
00072 /* Thread structure. */
00073 struct thread {
00074         /*
00075          * These go up front so they're easy to get to even if the
00076          * debugger is messed up.
00077          */
00078         char *t_name;                   /* Name of this thread */
00079         const char *t_wchan_name;       /* Name of wait channel, if sleeping */
00080         threadstate_t t_state;          /* State this thread is in */
00081 
00082         /*
00083          * Thread subsystem internal fields.
00084          */
00085         struct thread_machdep t_machdep; /* Any machine-dependent goo */
00086         struct threadlistnode t_listnode; /* Link for run/sleep/zombie lists */
00087         void *t_stack;                  /* Kernel-level stack */
00088         struct switchframe *t_context;  /* Saved register context (on stack) */
00089         struct cpu *t_cpu;              /* CPU thread runs on */
00090 
00091         /*
00092          * Interrupt state fields.
00093          *
00094          * t_in_interrupt is true if current execution is in an
00095          * interrupt handler, which means the thread's normal context
00096          * of execution is stopped somewhere in the middle of doing
00097          * something else. This makes assorted operations unsafe.
00098          *
00099          * See notes in spinlock.c regarding t_curspl and t_iplhigh_count.
00100          *
00101          * Exercise for the student: why is this material per-thread
00102          * rather than per-cpu or global?
00103          */
00104         bool t_in_interrupt;            /* Are we in an interrupt? */
00105         int t_curspl;                   /* Current spl*() state */
00106         int t_iplhigh_count;            /* # of times IPL has been raised */
00107 
00108         /*
00109          * Public fields
00110          */
00111 
00112         /* VM */
00113         struct addrspace *t_addrspace;  /* virtual address space */
00114 
00115         /* VFS */
00116         struct vnode *t_cwd;            /* current working directory */
00117 
00118         /* add more here as needed */
00119 
00120         /* Thread local open file table */
00121         struct file* table_fd[MAX_FD];
00122 
00123         /* Last file descriptor */
00124         int last_fd;
00125 
00126         /* Thread id */
00127         pid_t thread_id;
00128 
00129         /* Parent Thread id */
00130         pid_t parent_id;
00131 
00132         /* Exit code */
00133         int exit_code;
00134 
00135 };
00136 
00137 /* Call once during system startup to allocate data structures. */
00138 void thread_bootstrap(void);
00139 
00140 /* Call late in system startup to get secondary CPUs running. */
00141 void thread_start_cpus(void);
00142 
00143 /* Call during panic to stop other threads in their tracks */
00144 void thread_panic(void);
00145 
00146 /* Call during system shutdown to offline other CPUs. */
00147 void thread_shutdown(void);
00148 
00149 /*
00150  * Make a new thread, which will start executing at "func". The "data"
00151  * arguments (one pointer, one number) are passed to the function. The
00152  * current thread is used as a prototype for creating the new one. If
00153  * "ret" is non-null, the thread structure for the new thread is
00154  * handed back. (Note that using said thread structure from the parent
00155  * thread should be done only with caution, because in general the
00156  * child thread might exit at any time.) Returns an error code.
00157  */
00158 int thread_fork(const char *name, 
00159                 void (*func)(void *, unsigned long),
00160                 void *data1, unsigned long data2, 
00161                 struct thread **ret);
00162 
00163 /*
00164  * Cause the current thread to exit.
00165  * Interrupts need not be disabled.
00166  */
00167 void thread_exit(void);
00168 
00169 /*
00170  * Cause the current thread to yield to the next runnable thread, but
00171  * itself stay runnable.
00172  * Interrupts need not be disabled.
00173  */
00174 void thread_yield(void);
00175 
00176 /*
00177  * Reshuffle the run queue. Called from the timer interrupt.
00178  */
00179 void schedule(void);
00180 
00181 /*
00182  * Potentially migrate ready threads to other CPUs. Called from the
00183  * timer interrupt.
00184  */
00185 void thread_consider_migration(void);
00186 
00187 /* Find next pid */
00188 int find_nextpid(void);
00189 pid_t thread_fork2(struct trapframe* tf, int *retval);
00190 
00191 #endif /* _THREAD_H_ */
Generated on Sun Jun 27 22:12:13 2010 for Os/161 by  1.6.1
